revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Module:Unicode convert]] <includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Module:Unicode convert]]</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: hopefully last batch of imports
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Module:Unicode convert]] <includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Module:Unicode convert]]</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: remove deleted category New automatic wikipedia redirects

revision.text:
local p = {}
-- NOTE: all these functions use frame solely for its args member. -- Modules using them may therefore call them with a fake frame table -- containing only args.
p.getUTF8 = function (frame) local ch = mw.ustring.char(tonumber(frame.args[1] or '0', 16) or 0) local bytes = {mw.ustring.byte(ch, 1, -1)} local format = ({ ['10'] = '%d', dec = '%d' })[frame.args['base']] or '%02X' for i = 1, #bytes do bytes[i] = format:format(bytes[i]) end return table.concat(bytes, ' ') end
p.getUTF16 = function (frame) local codepoint = tonumber(frame.args[1] or '0', 16) or 0 local format = ({ -- TODO reduce the number of options. ['10'] = '%d', dec = '%d' })[frame.args['base']] or '%04X' if codepoint <= 0xFFFF then -- NB this also returns lone surrogate characters return format:format(codepoint) elseif codepoint > 0x10FFFF then -- There are no codepoints above this return '' end codepoint = codepoint - 0x10000 bit32 = require('bit32') return (format .. ' ' .. format):format( bit32.rshift(codepoint, 10) + 0xD800, bit32.band(codepoint, 0x3FF) + 0xDC00) end
p.fromUTF8 = function(frame) local basein = frame.args['basein'] == 'dec' and 10 or 16 local format = frame.args['base'] == 'dec' and '%d ' or '%02X ' local bytes = {} for byte in mw.text.gsplit(frame.args[1], '%s') do table.insert(bytes, tonumber(byte, basein)) end local chars = {mw.ustring.codepoint(string.char(unpack(bytes)), 1, -1)} return format:rep(#chars):sub(1, -2):format(unpack(chars)) end
return p

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: trying to do a last fix of these, they will be fixed and not bloat the site
revision.text:
local p = {}
-- NOTE: all these functions use frame solely for its args member. -- Modules using them may therefore call them with a fake frame table -- containing only args.
p.getUTF8 = function (frame) local ch = mw.ustring.char(tonumber(frame.args[1] or '0', 16) or 0) local bytes = {mw.ustring.byte(ch, 1, -1)} local format = ({ ['10'] = '%d', dec = '%d' })[frame.args['base']] or '%02X' for i = 1, #bytes do bytes[i] = format:format(bytes[i]) end return table.concat(bytes, ' ') end
p.getUTF16 = function (frame) local codepoint = tonumber(frame.args[1] or '0', 16) or 0 local format = ({ -- TODO reduce the number of options. ['10'] = '%d', dec = '%d' })[frame.args['base']] or '%04X' if codepoint <= 0xFFFF then -- NB this also returns lone surrogate characters return format:format(codepoint) elseif codepoint > 0x10FFFF then -- There are no codepoints above this return '' end codepoint = codepoint - 0x10000 bit32 = require('bit32') return (format .. ' ' .. format):format( bit32.rshift(codepoint, 10) + 0xD800, bit32.band(codepoint, 0x3FF) + 0xDC00) end
p.fromUTF8 = function(frame) local basein = frame.args['basein'] == 'dec' and 10 or 16 local format = frame.args['base'] == 'dec' and '%d ' or '%02X ' local bytes = {} for byte in mw.text.gsplit(frame.args[1], '%s') do table.insert(bytes, tonumber(byte, basein)) end local chars = {mw.ustring.codepoint(string.char(unpack(bytes)), 1, -1)} return format:rep(#chars):sub(1, -2):format(unpack(chars)) end
return p
